picks.original_reason: Both Rodón and Mize carry [sm] flags, which widens the true-talent band enough to keep the side below my 3.5-pt gate. My run estimate is ~9.3, giving a 1.8-run edge to the Over, but dual small-sample variance caps the total at a lean.

Luzardo’s 3.18 xFIP against Littell’s 5.62 xFIP and exhausted Washington bullpen (0.5) gives the Over 8.5 a 2.4-run edge worth 1 unit. The side edge is ~3.0 pts after penalizing Littell’s [sm], so it is only a lean.

Luzardo’s 3.18 xFIP against Littell’s 5.62 xFIP and exhausted Washington bullpen (0.5) gives the Over 8.5 a 2.4-run edge worth 1 unit. The side edge is ~3.0 pts after penalizing Littell’s [sm], so it is only a lean.

Messick’s 3.44 xFIP and Cleveland’s fresh bullpen (3.25) give a raw 4.0-pt edge, but both starters carry [sm] flags that shave the margin to a lean once the wider confidence band is honored. The total is priced at my estimate.
picks.cc_cited_fact: Messick’s 3.44 xFIP and Cleveland’s fresh bullpen (3.25) remain intact; confirmed lineups show no scratches and identical platoon splits (CLE 95 wRC+ vs RHP, CHW 121 wRC+ vs LHP).

picks.original_reason: Coors Field side bets are excluded by method due to extreme margin-of-victory variance. My park-adjusted total is ~13.1, but Sullivan’s [sm] and the high-altitude volatility make the Over a lean only.
picks.cc_cited_fact: BOS aggregate vs-LHP wRC+ confirmed unchanged at 124

picks.original_reason: Baz’s 4.39 xFIP against Johnson’s 7.00 [sm] xFIP creates a 4.5-pt win-probability gap, and Baltimore’s fresh leverage arms (2.75) support late-game stability. Johnson’s small-sample uncertainty caps the stake at 1 unit.
picks.cc_cited_fact: BAL vs RHP team wRC+ 103 (n=9) and LAA vs RHP team wRC+ 97 (n=8) unchanged from Run-1; no lineup scratches; ML unchanged at -147
